rulings.Case Title: MONICA HUJAZI, AN INDIVIDUIAL AND TRUSTEE OF THE VS. SYDNEY A. LUSCUTOFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Sydney Luscutoff And Luscutoff, Lendormy & Associates' Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiffs And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, July 29, 2013, Line 1: Defendants SYDNEY LUSCUTOFF'S and LUSCUTOFF, LENDORMY & ASSOCIATES' Motion To Compel Discovery Responses from Plaintiffs and Request for Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. It would be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to address whether Plaintiff Monica Hujazi has responded in her individual capacity to Defendants' Form Interrogatories - Set One and Special Interrogatories - Set One, and to be prepared to address their positions on the status of Plaintiff's production of documents in response to Defendants' Demand for Production- Set One. Defendants' motion appears to be moot with respect to Defendants' Requests for Admissions - Set One. It would also be helpful to the court for the parties to be prepared to address their positions on the merits under the Code of Civil Procedure of Defendants' request for sanctions. The filing of an involuntary bankruptcy petition against Plaintiff does not preclude a state court from awarding discovery sanctions. Keitel v. Heubel (2002) 103 Cal. App. 4th 324, 334. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN V ANDREWS et al VS. TETRA TECH EC, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 29, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT NEW WORLD ENVIRONMENTAL, INC. D/B/A NEW WORLD TECHNOLOGY's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. Plaintiff Jahr has pled sufficient facts to allege retaliatory termination against Defendant New World Environmental, Inc. dba New World Technology ("Defendant"). The Court does not lack jurisdiction; the Labor Commission has suspended its investigation in light of the filing of this action. (Second Am. Complaint, 37, Exhibit A.) A claim under Labor Code § 6310 does not require the exhaustion of administrative remedies. (Lab. Code § 6312.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARY WITH VS. JOSEPH ENGLERT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 29, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANTs JOSEPH ENGLERT, EXPORT ASSIST, INC., EXPORT ASSIST INTERNATIONAL, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the first cause of action for intentional misrepresentation cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff pleads sufficient facts to support the claim, paragraph 23 does not specify when the cause of action accrued and paragraphs 36-39 sufficiently allege the discovery rule. The demurrer to the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that she was an investor at paragraphs 21 and 29 and that relationship can create a fiduciary relationship. See Cleveland v. Johnson (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 1315, 1339. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 4th cause of action. Civil Code § 1714.01 does not create a distinct cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress and the tort is negligence. See Burgess v. Sup. Crt. (1992) 2 Cal.4th 1064, 1072. The demurrer to the 5th cause of action for negligence is overruled. Plaintiff alleges a breach of duty. The demurrer to the 6th cause of action for conversion is overruled. Paragraphs 91 and 92 sufficiently allege wrongful dominion because defendants failed to return plaintiff's property. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ILONA BRUSIL VS. NIKOLAY BUSEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, July 29, 2013, Line 13, CROSS DEFENDANT VLADIMIR RUBASHEVSKY's Amended Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Motion for good faith settlement granted. Substantial evidence supports a finding of good faith settlement based on the factors set forth in Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associatiates (1985) 38 Cal. 3d. 488. Plaintiff seeks approximately $40,000 in damages. Settlors' payment of $10,000 is in the ball park. There is insufficient evidence of collusion. The request for discovery is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
